CORRECTION 1 — STREAMING BUGFIX (Bodø/Glimt CL-qualifier, both legs). The two events.json rows still carried `streaming: [{TV 2 Play}]` from the 14:30Z run even though their summaries + tracked.json reason had already been corrected — i.e. the field the dashboard RENDERS as the channel was wrong (a «hvor kan jeg se det» correctness defect). Fixed: HOME (Aspmyra 11 Aug) → «Direktesport / Avisa Nordland» (direktesport.no); AWAY (Brussel 4 Aug) → empty (honest «–»). AN's home-qualifier rights re-confirmed this run against an.no («AN viser denne runden, samt runde tre» = covers the CL third qualifying-round home match; away rights bought separately per opponent, unconfirmed). The one validate-events streaming-missing warning is exactly this deliberate empty away leg.

CORRECTION 2 — TOUR DE FRANCE STAGE 17 (now finished; cycling web-scout). Jasper Philipsen (Alpecin–Deceuninck) won the bunch sprint from a ~40-rider break (Mathieu van der Poel lead-out) — his first stage win of this Tour; Mauro Schmid 2nd, Olav Kooij 3rd. No Norwegian on the podium, no Norwegian withdrawals. GC unchanged at the top (confirmed across cyclinguptodate + cyclingnews + idlprocycling): Pogačar yellow+polka-dot, Evenepoel +4.32, del Toro +6.51 (white), Seixas +7.11, Ayuso +9.22; green Mads Pedersen (417 pts, now leads Philipsen by 31 after the stage win); best Norwegian Tobias Halland Johannessen ~16th. Amended stage-17 summary (result) + stage-18 «Nå» summary (fresh GC line). Stage 18 (Voiron→Orcières-Merlette, mountain, Thu 23) start 10.35 UTC re-confirmed — first decisive Alpine day.

RIGHTS CORRECTION for norwegian-rights SKILL.md (Edit attempted, PERMISSION-GATED in this interactive session — the gate is empirically real here despite the WP-91 note; a CI run without it should transcribe): (1) Champions League incl. qualifying = TV 2 Play through 2026/27 (TV 2 holds ONLY the CL of the UEFA club pack); Europa League + Conference League incl. qualifying = VIAPLAY through 2031 — corrects the old «Champions League / Europa League → TV 2 Play» line (nettavisen.no/sport/viaplay-bekrefter-ny-fotballavtale; re-confirmed via Brann Conference-qualifier on tvkampen). (2) UEFA Super Cup = TV 2 Play. (3) NUANCE: a Norwegian club's UEFA qualifier can sit with a LOCAL rights-holder — Bodø/Glimt's CL/EL/Conference qualifying HOME matches 2024–2026 on Avisa Nordland + Direktesport (except playoff round); away qualifiers negotiated separately → verify per match.
